Description: Stand up the External ID (CIAM) tenant and configure sign-up/sign-in, MFA, branding and admin protections so the app delegates all credential security to Microsoft.
Mobile and operator login experience built on External ID.
Auth-provider-agnostic business API for permits, visits, sightings, hazards, locks and notifications. Auth enforced at APIM; no identity/auth tables.
Core mobile app experience and self-service.
Site map, birdwatching areas and wayfinding.
Safety, site info and tours managed by operators.
Electronic gate access replacing physical keys.
Push notifications for hazards, closures and evacuation.
Web dashboard for operators to manage users and the site.
IaC, networking, APIM, CI/CD and edge (Cloudflare + Front Door).
Integration, UAT, security and performance validation.

As a developer I want to validate the MSAL + External ID sign-up/sign-in flow on iOS and Android so that we confirm the token flow before committing to the full build. Time-box 3 days. Done when: spike branch demonstrates MSAL sign-up, sign-in, token acquisition and silent refresh; PKCE + Email OTP confirmed against a test External ID tenant; findings, risks and recommended approach documented.

As an IDAM admin I want an external (CIAM) tenant provisioned so that public users are isolated from the MW workforce tenant. AC: RG rg-hub-ase-extid created in 0.MelbWater-Shared-Svcs and tagged; external tenant melbwaterext.onmicrosoft.com created with data residency Australia (immutable); MAU billing linked with budget + alert; tenant reachable via Entra admin centre and https://melbwaterext.ciamlogin.com.

As a developer I want mobile, API and operator app registrations per environment so that no app objects are shared across dev/UAT/prod. AC: birdwatcher-mobile-<env> (public/native PKCE), birdwatcher-api-<env> (exposes Birdwatcher.Operations.ReadWrite), birdwatcher-operator-<env> registered per env; redirect URIs configured; no shared registrations.

Summary: User flows (sign-up / sign-in / SSPR / profile edit)
Description: As a public user I want sign-up, sign-in, password reset and profile edit flows so that I can self-serve without operator involvement. AC: Email + OTP baseline; sign-up collects displayName, vehicleRego, accountApproved; SSPR works end-to-end with no operator involvement; MW branding applied at tenant level.

Summary: Admin security (MFA PIM Conditional Access break-glass)
Description: As a security lead I want privileged roles protected so that admin access is least-privilege and monitored. AC: MFA enforced on all admin roles; PIM eligible assignments; CA requires MFA for admin sign-ins while end users get risk-based step-up only (no device controls); break-glass cloud-only accounts excluded from CA blocking with sign-in alerting.

Summary: Custom URL domain via Azure Front Door
Description: As a user I want a branded login domain (login.melbournewater.com.au) so that the sign-in experience is trusted and on-brand. AC: custom domain verified (TXT) and associated as custom URL domain; Front Door profile created (origin <tenant>.ciamlogin.com, WAF/caching empty per MS guidance), managed TLS, route enabled; MSAL config + redirect URIs updated; full flow validated through the custom domain.

As a new public user I want to sign up in the app so that I can access permit and site features. AC: Sign up launches the External ID sign-up flow; Email OTP verification succeeds; profile attributes captured and tokens returned; first authenticated API call to Operations API succeeds with the bearer token.

Summary: Returning user sign-in + silent token refresh
Description: As a returning user I want to stay signed in so that I'm not re-prompted every session. AC: cached account signs in silently; MFA challenged only when risk requires it; refresh token acquires access tokens silently for subsequent API calls.
As a user I want to reset my password in-app so that I don't need operator help. AC: Forgot password launches SSPR; email-verified reset completes with no operator involvement.

As a member of the public I want to use maps and site info without logging in so that I can explore before applying. AC: app launches with maps and general site info available with no login; protected features prompt for sign-in only when needed.

As an operator I want user profile and permit-state data so that I can see who holds access and its validity. AC: CRUD for UserProfiles (user details, permit type long/short, permit state); existing ~3K users importable (no OID); new users linkable on first sign-in; OpenAPI documented; APIM enforces validate-jwt.
As an operator I want to know who is on site so that I can respond to safety events. AC: endpoints record site entry/exit and current presence; operator query returns current on-site list.

As a birdwatcher I want to report sightings, hazards and injured wildlife so that operators are informed. AC: create/list endpoints for sightings, hazards and issue reports (optional location/photo ref); operator state transitions (acknowledged/resolved) supported.

As a developer I want endpoints for tours, locks and notifications so that the app and admin features have a data source. AC: CRUD for tour content references, lock registry and notification records; schema covers permits, visits, sightings, hazards, tours, locks, notifications with no auth tables.

As a platform engineer I want APIM to validate External ID tokens so that the API trusts the gateway only. AC: validate-jwt targets External ID OIDC issuer (RS256), audience birdwatcher-api; rate limiting and Named Values configured; API does not validate tokens itself.
As a birdwatcher I want to update my details in-app so that my information stays current. AC: profile edits persist via the profile-edit flow / Operations API; vehicle registration editable.
As a visitor I want to read safety induction info so that I understand site hazards before visiting. AC: induction content viewable; completion state recorded where required.

As a visitor I want introductory info and trip-planning so that I can plan my visit. AC: intro content and planning views render; deep-link to permit application (short-term redirect / long-term in-app) works.
As a visitor I want to report an issue with optional photo and location so that operators can act. AC: issue submitted to Operations API; user sees confirmation and status.
As a user I want an interactive WTP map so that I can see birdwatching areas and hazards. AC: map renders offline-tolerant base layer; birdwatching areas and gates shown.
As a visitor I want to navigate to birdwatching areas so that I can move around the large site safely. AC: route guidance to selected area; current location displayed; hazard zones highlighted.
As an operator I want to update map points (areas, hazards, gates) so that the map stays accurate. AC: operator edits to map features reflect in the app after sync.
As an operator I want to create/edit safety and site content so that visitors see current information. AC: CRUD for content items; publish/unpublish; versioning of published content.
As an operator I want to author guided tours so that visitors get a self-guided experience. AC: tour steps (text, media, map points) authored and published; app renders the tour.
As an operator I want to upload and manage media so that content includes images/audio. AC: media uploaded to blob storage; referenced by content; size/type validated.
As an operator I want to register Bluetooth locks and assign access so that approved visitors can open relevant gates. AC: locks registered; access grants tied to permit/area; revocation supported.

As a visitor I want to open an authorised gate from the app so that I don't need a physical key. AC: app opens gate only when authorised; unlock event recorded; failure handled gracefully when out of range or unauthorised.
As an operator I want lock/access events logged so that I know who entered and when. AC: unlock events feed visit/presence data; operator can review access history.
As a platform engineer I want Notification Hubs configured so that push works on iOS and Android. AC: Shared Services namespace; dev + prod hubs; device registration on sign-in.

As an operator I want to send real-time alerts so that visitors are warned of hazards or told to evacuate. AC: operator sends targeted/broadcast push; delivery status visible; evacuation alert prioritised.
As a visitor I want to see and manage notifications so that I don't miss important alerts. AC: notification history viewable; permission prompts handled; safety alerts cannot be silently lost.

As an operator I want to sign in to the web dashboard with my MW account so that access is governed by MW IDAM. AC: operator app registration + app roles (WTP.Operator, WTP.Admin); HTTPS redirect URIs; role-gated UI.

As an operator I want to manage user accounts and permits so that I can onboard and maintain access holders. AC: create new user sends registration + app-download link; update/renew/cancel permits; long/short term handled.
As an operator I want to see who is on site so that I can respond to incidents. AC: live on-site list; issue/hazard reports queue; links to broadcast alerts.
As an operator I want to review and action access applications so that approvals are tracked. AC: application list with approve/reject; status reflected to user; payment/permit type captured.

As a platform engineer I want parameterised IaC so that all environments deploy consistently. AC: VNet/subnets/NSGs, Azure Firewall policy, APIM, App Service VNet integration + private endpoints, Azure SQL all parameterised per env.

As a platform engineer I want the two edges configured so that the API and auth planes are protected appropriately. AC: Cloudflare on API path (OWASP Core, Authenticated Origin Pulls, origin IP lock); Front Door branded proxy on auth path (WAF/caching empty).

As a platform engineer I want the border-firewall change requests prepared so that the network path is approved per env. AC: Cloudflare IP ranges/ports documented; ExpressRoute gateway confirmed; BGP advertises APIM subnet; end-to-end smoke test passes.

As a developer I want CI/CD for IaC, API, Static Web App and DB so that deployments are repeatable via managed identity. AC: pipelines deploy each component to dev/UAT/prod; secrets via Key Vault / managed identity; no Identity API pipeline.

As a platform engineer I want monitoring and secrets management so that the platform is observable and secure. AC: App Insights + Log Analytics; alerts configured; no JWT signing key stored (Microsoft-managed RS256).

As a QA engineer I want to test full auth + API flows so that the app and backend integrate correctly. AC: mock tokens replaced with real External ID tokens; all Operations API calls exercised; bug-fix cycle complete.

As a QA engineer I want UAT and promotion smoke tests so that releases are validated end to end. AC: smoke tests pass across the full Cloudflare to firewall to ExpressRoute to Azure FW to APIM to API path in each env.
As a security lead I want security testing so that the solution meets MW cyber requirements. AC: OWASP-aligned checks on API; auth-flow abuse cases; findings triaged and remediated.
As a QA engineer I want load and resilience tests so that the platform handles expected volume. AC: target load sustained within SLAs; failover/availability behaviour validated.
